Getting Started Guide

Claude Code
Zero to First Project

Installation, setup, and 50 professional skills ready to use. A complete guide for beginners.

Free Gift from Focus AI

50 Professional Claude Code Skills, Free

A ready-to-install bundle with 50 professional skills: code review, security audit, debugging, testing, git workflow and more. Installed in one command.

Download Skills Bundle (ZIP)
claude-code-getting-started-en.md

What Is Claude Code?

Claude Code is Anthropic's official CLI — an AI assistant that lives inside your terminal. Unlike browser-based tools, Claude Code can read your files, write code, run commands, and edit your project directly.

Think of it as a senior developer sitting next to you, available 24/7.

Prerequisites

RequirementWhy
macOS / Linux / WindowsClaude Code runs in the terminal
Node.js 18+Claude Code is a Node.js package
Anthropic API keyClaude Code uses the API to talk to Claude

Check if Node.js is installed:

node --version

If you see v18.x.x or higher, you're set. If not, download from nodejs.org (LTS version).

1 Get Your Anthropic API Key

  1. Go to console.anthropic.com
  2. Sign up or log in
  3. Click "Get API Keys" in the left sidebar
  4. Click "Create Key" → name it anything (e.g., "claude-code")
  5. Copy the key (starts with sk-ant-...)
  6. Save it somewhere safe — you won't be able to see it again
Billing note: Claude Code uses API credits. New accounts get free credits. After that, usage is pay-as-you-go, typically $2 to $10/month for regular developer use.

2 Install Claude Code

npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

Verify the installation:

claude --version

You should see a version number like 1.x.x.

3 Authenticate

claude

The first time you run this, Claude Code will ask for your API key. Paste the key from Step 1 and press Enter.

On Windows, if claude isn't recognized, try opening a new terminal window after installation.

4 Install Your Skills Bundle

Unzip the bundle and open its folder in a terminal.

macOS / Linux

bash install.sh

Windows (PowerShell)

.\install.ps1

The installer places all 50 skills in the correct location.

Verify installation:

# macOS / Linux ls ~/.claude/skills/ # Windows dir $env:USERPROFILE\.claude\skills

You should see 50 skill folders listed.

5 Your First Project

5.1 Create a project folder

mkdir my-first-project cd my-first-project

5.2 Start Claude Code

claude

You'll see Claude's greeting and a > prompt.

5.3 Try your first commands

# Explore an existing codebase > /onboard-codebase # Plan a feature > /task-breakdown Add user authentication with JWT tokens # Code review > /code-review # Write a commit message > /commit-message

How to Use Skills

Skills are invoked with a / prefix:

> /skill-name [optional argument]

Some skills work on their own. Others need context:

> /fix-error TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map') > /debug-smart The API call returns 200 but the UI doesn't update > /explain-code [paste the function you want explained]

All 50 Skills, By Category

Code Quality

/code-reviewSystematic review with severity levels
/security-reviewOWASP-based security audit
/performance-reviewFind N+1, memory leaks, slow patterns
/refactorIdentify and fix code smells
/clean-codeNaming, functions, error handling reference
/solid-principlesSOLID principles with examples
/tech-debtCatalog and prioritize technical debt
/accessibilityWCAG 2.1 AA checklist

Debugging

/debug-smartScientific debugging method
/fix-errorError decoder + stack trace reading
/trace-bugFive Whys root cause analysis
/memory-leakDetect and fix memory leaks
/async-debugDiagnose Promise and async/await bugs
/typescript-errorsDecode TS2345, TS2322 and more
/regex-helperRegex reference + common pitfalls

Documentation

/generate-docsWrite docs for any code
/jsdocJSDoc/TSDoc tag reference
/readme-writeWrite a proper README
/api-docsDocument REST APIs
/changelogGenerate changelogs from git history
/explain-codeExplain code at 3 audience levels

Testing

/write-testsWrite unit tests (AAA pattern)
/edge-casesFind edge cases you missed
/mock-generateGenerate test mocks and fakes
/test-coverageAnalyze coverage gaps
/e2e-testsPlaywright/Cypress test patterns
/test-dataGenerate realistic test data
/snapshot-testsWhen and how to use snapshots

Git & Workflow

/commit-messageConventional Commits from staged diff
/pr-descriptionPR description from git log
/git-cleanupClean stale branches and history
/merge-conflictResolve merge conflicts systematically
/release-notesUser-facing release notes
/branch-strategyBranching strategy, naming conventions, PR sizing

Architecture

/architecture-reviewDeep review with ADR output
/design-patternsDesign pattern reference: which pattern to use and when
/api-designREST and GraphQL best practices
/database-schemaSchema design and migration patterns
/microservicesService boundaries and distributed systems
/dependency-auditSecurity and hygiene audit for packages
/folder-structureProject organization by framework

Security

/security-auditFull OWASP Top 10 audit
/env-securitySecrets and environment variable hygiene
/sql-injectionSQL injection prevention reference
/auth-reviewJWT, sessions, RBAC patterns
/csp-setupContent Security Policy configuration

Productivity

/standupPrepare daily standup notes
/task-breakdownDecompose features into tasks
/onboard-codebaseRapid codebase exploration report
/sprint-planSprint planning with capacity check

Tips

  • Be specific. "Fix the bug" gives Claude nothing to work with. "Fix the bug in calculateTotal — it returns NaN when the cart is empty" — that works.
  • Give context. Before a code review, describe what the code does. Before debugging, describe what you expected vs. what happened.
  • Use it for learning. Ask Claude to explain decisions: "Why did you use a closure here?" or "What's the trade-off between these two approaches?"
  • Chain skills. Write feature → /code-review → /write-tests → /commit-message → /pr-description. A complete professional workflow in minutes.
  • Review everything. Claude Code is powerful but not infallible. Read the code before committing — you're the engineer responsible for the outcome.

Keyboard Shortcuts

ShortcutAction
Ctrl+CCancel current operation / stop generation
Ctrl+DExit Claude Code
Ctrl+LClear screen (history preserved)
↑ / ↓Navigate command history
Ctrl+RInteractive reverse search through history
EscapeCancel current input / clear line
Escape EscapeRewind conversation to previous point
Ctrl+BRun current task in background
Ctrl+OToggle verbose output (show tool details)

Built-in slash commands: /help · /clear · /cost · /model · /compact

Troubleshooting

"claude: command not found" — Node.js global bin isn't in your PATH. Try npx @anthropic-ai/claude-code, or reinstall Node.js from nodejs.org.

"Invalid API key" — The key may have been copied incorrectly. Go to console.anthropic.com and create a new one.

"Rate limit exceeded" — You've hit the API rate limit. Wait a minute and try again.

Skills not working — Verify that ~/.claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md exists. If not, run install.sh again.
